predictions.title: Big-3 hyperscalers ship MCP-server policy enforcement as default by Q4 2026
predictions.body: **By Q4 2026 the Microsoft Agent 365 GA ($15/user, M365 E7) + Cisco DefenseClaw open-source (Mar 23) + CSAI Foundation MITRE-CNA (Apr 29) + AWS Bedrock AgentCore Managed Harness (Apr 28) stack converges into an enterprise agent-control-plane reference model — at least 2 of the Big-3 hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) ship policy-based MCP-server allow/block enforcement at the tenant level with audited tool-invocation logs as a default-on primitive; at least one independent control-plane vendor (Vanta, Zenity, OpenClawAI) publishes a Sec-Labels-style artifact attestation gate.** Forcing function is enterprise procurement: Microsoft just put a per-user price tag ($15/user/mo) on the agent-control-plane category, which sets the pricing-anchor that AWS + Google must respond to with comparable disclosures; the Apr 30 OpenAI Agents SDK + 7 sandbox providers pre-baked the runtime side, and the May 1 GA wires the policy side to an existing M365 admin surface (Entra/Purview/Intune/Defender). Once the May 1 GA's Defender asset-context mapping (devices + MCP servers + identities + cloud resources per agent) ships in June 2026 it becomes the de-facto schema; the path of least resistance is for AWS Bedrock AgentCore + Google Vertex Agent Garden to publish parallel schemas before Q4 to avoid losing the M365-installed-base anchor. CSAI's MITRE-CNA cadence will compress the disclosure side in parallel. In plain language: Microsoft put a price tag on controlling AI agents — other clouds will copy or lose customers. In plain language: In plain language:
predictions.summary: **By Q4 2026 the Microsoft Agent 365 GA ($15/user, M365 E7) + Cisco DefenseClaw open-source (Mar 23) + CSAI Foundation MITRE-CNA (Apr 29) + AWS Bedrock AgentCore Managed Harness (Apr 28) stack converges into an enterprise agent-control-plane reference model — at least 2 of the Big-3 hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) ship policy-based MCP-server allow/block enforcement at the tenant level with audited tool-invocation logs as a default-on primitive; at least one independent control-plane vendor (Vanta, Zenity, OpenClawAI) publishes a Sec-Labels-style artifact attestation gate.** Forcing function is enterprise procurement: Microsoft just put a per-user price tag ($15/user/mo) on the agent-control-plane category, which sets the pricing-anchor that AWS + Google must respond to with comparable disclosures; the Apr 30 OpenAI Agents SDK + 7 sandbox providers pre-baked the runtime side, and the May 1 GA wires the policy side to an existing M365 admin surface (Entra/Purview/Intune/Defender).

predictions.title: LLM-tooling + Linux-kernel CVE chain becomes dominant attack pattern by H2 2026
predictions.body: **By H2 2026 the May 1 Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431) BOD-22-01 14-day deadline + the Apr 30 LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 26-hour exploitation window + the unpatched-into-May-1 SGLang CVE-2026-5760 trio makes "supply-chain weaponization of LLM-tooling-layer CVEs" the dominant attacker pattern — at least one major IR firm publishes a public post-mortem of a real intrusion that pairs an LLM-gateway / agent-harness / inference-runtime CVE with a Linux-kernel LPE (Copy Fail or its successor) for full-chain root-on-multi-tenant-inference, and at least one CSAI-CNA-issued advisory explicitly carves out the LLM-tooling + kernel chain as a named technique within ATT&CK v19's Defense Impairment.** Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 already names the agentic techniques (context poisoning, tool invocation abuse, MCP server compromise, malicious agent deployment); the May 1 Copy Fail wave gives attackers a universal-Linux LPE with confirmed in-the-wild use against cloud service providers (per Microsoft Security Blog disclosure); the LiteLLM SQLi-row-leaks-OpenAI/Anthropic/Bedrock-keys precedent gives them the lateral-movement vector. CSAI's Apr 29 CNA authorization is the disclosure-issuance side; the path of least resistance for an IR firm Q3 incident report is one cloud-multi-tenant intrusion stitched together this exact way. In plain language: Hackers can chain an AI-tooling bug with a Linux-kernel bug to take over shared AI servers. In plain language: In plain language:
predictions.summary: **By H2 2026 the May 1 Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431) BOD-22-01 14-day deadline + the Apr 30 LiteLLM CVE-2026-42208 26-hour exploitation window + the unpatched-into-May-1 SGLang CVE-2026-5760 trio makes "supply-chain weaponization of LLM-tooling-layer CVEs" the dominant attacker pattern — at least one major IR firm publishes a public post-mortem of a real intrusion that pairs an LLM-gateway / agent-harness / inference-runtime CVE with a Linux-kernel LPE (Copy Fail or its successor) for full-chain root-on-multi-tenant-inference, and at least one CSAI-CNA-issued advisory explicitly carves out the LLM-tooling + kernel chain as a named technique within ATT&CK v19's Defense Impairment.** Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 already names the agentic techniques (context poisoning, tool invocation abuse, MCP server compromise, malicious agent deployment); the May 1 Copy Fail wave gives attackers a universal-Linux LPE with confirmed in-the-wild use against cloud service providers (per Microsoft Security Blog disclosure); the LiteLLM SQLi-row-leaks-OpenAI/Anthropic/Bedrock-keys precedent gives them the lateral-movement vector.

predictions.title: Two more frontier labs publish autonomous-weapons carve-outs by Q3 2026
predictions.body: **The May 1 Pentagon-snubs-Anthropic + 7-vendor classified-network AI deal becomes the first US presidential-administration vs frontier-AI-lab autonomous-weapons-use line in the sand — by Q3 2026 at least two other frontier labs (Mistral, OpenAI, xAI, Google DeepMind, Inflection-2026, Cohere) explicitly publish a use-of-force / autonomous-weapons carve-out in their public ToS or DoD bid responses; Anthropic's federal-judge-blocked Trump-admin freeze (April CA ruling) becomes the operative procurement-law precedent; Q3 earnings calls add an "autonomous-weapons exposure" disclosure line for the cohort.** Forcing function is litigation precedent: the federal-judge block already exists, so any future DoD freeze action against a competing lab now has a court-tested predicate. Trump's "deal is possible" framing on Apr 17 + the May 1 contract signings are the carrot-and-stick choreography; once a deal-or-no-deal binary becomes visible to the buyside, capital-allocation pressure will force every frontier-lab board to formally adopt either the Anthropic posture (carve-out, accept exclusion) or the OpenAI/Microsoft/Google posture (no carve-out, accept full DoD use). The middle position becomes untenable as soon as Q3 earnings cycle starts. In plain language: Anthropic's Pentagon snub forces every other AI lab to pick: take the contract or refuse it. In plain language: In plain language:
predictions.summary: **The May 1 Pentagon-snubs-Anthropic + 7-vendor classified-network AI deal becomes the first US presidential-administration vs frontier-AI-lab autonomous-weapons-use line in the sand — by Q3 2026 at least two other frontier labs (Mistral, OpenAI, xAI, Google DeepMind, Inflection-2026, Cohere) explicitly publish a use-of-force / autonomous-weapons carve-out in their public ToS or DoD bid responses; Anthropic's federal-judge-blocked Trump-admin freeze (April CA ruling) becomes the operative procurement-law precedent; Q3 earnings calls add an "autonomous-weapons exposure" disclosure line for the cohort.** Forcing function is litigation precedent: the federal-judge block already exists, so any future DoD freeze action against a competing lab now has a court-tested predicate.

predictions.title: Cerebras IPO mid-May prices above $22-25B with first-day pop ≥30%
predictions.body: **The May 1 record S&P 7,230.12 + Nasdaq 25,114.44 + April best-month-since-2020 + AAPL +3.28% to $280.25 + $700B 2026 Big-Tech AI-capex tape sets up the mid-May Cerebras IPO to price at or above the top of the $22-25B targeted range with a multi-day pop — the Apr 30 Apple +$100B buyback authorization + the Q3 guide (+14-17%) is the buyside ratification of the AI-capex-on-faith thesis, and the Cerebras roadshow lands into the cleanest single tape since 2020; pricing closer to the pre-IPO secondary $26-28B than the public S-1 $22-25B; first-day pop ≥30% with the underwriter syndicate (Morgan Stanley / Citi / Barclays / UBS) pulling allocation aggressively from the long-only side; the IPO becomes the cohort signal that the AI-infrastructure-build is fundable through public markets and not just private capex.** $510M 2025 revenue + $20B+/750MW OpenAI deal through 2028 + the 24% G42 / 62% MBZUAI revenue concentration is priced in already; the swing factor is the May 1 record-tape ratification of the AI-capex thesis, which the buyside has been waiting for since the Apr 28 OpenAI-revenue-miss WSJ. The mid-May window also avoids competing with the May 8 Apr Jobs print + the May 5 AMD print; pricing should land in the pre-Cerebras quiet-period sweet spot. In plain language: A clean record stock tape makes the next AI-chip IPO likely to price above expectations. In plain language: In plain language:
predictions.summary: **The May 1 record S&P 7,230.12 + Nasdaq 25,114.44 + April best-month-since-2020 + AAPL +3.28% to $280.25 + $700B 2026 Big-Tech AI-capex tape sets up the mid-May Cerebras IPO to price at or above the top of the $22-25B targeted range with a multi-day pop — the Apr 30 Apple +$100B buyback authorization + the Q3 guide (+14-17%) is the buyside ratification of the AI-capex-on-faith thesis, and the Cerebras roadshow lands into the cleanest single tape since 2020; pricing closer to the pre-IPO secondary $26-28B than the public S-1 $22-25B; first-day pop ≥30% with the underwriter syndicate (Morgan Stanley / Citi / Barclays / UBS) pulling allocation aggressively from the long-only side; the IPO becomes the cohort signal that the AI-infrastructure-build is fundable through public markets and not just private capex.** $510M 2025 revenue + $20B+/750MW OpenAI deal through 2028 + the 24% G42 / 62% MBZUAI revenue concentration is priced in already; the swing factor is the May 1 record-tape ratification of the AI-capex thesis, which the buyside has been waiting for since the Apr 28 OpenAI-revenue-miss WSJ.
